questions.text: Is zero a rational number? Can you write it in the form p/q, where p and q are integers and q ≠ 0?
questions.correctAnswer: Yes, zero is a rational number. It can be written as 0/1, 0/2, 0/3, etc.
questions.explanation: Zero is a rational number because it can be expressed as p/q where p=0 and q is any non-zero integer.

questions.text: Find six rational numbers between 3 and 4.
questions.explanation: Between any two rational numbers, there exist infinitely many rational numbers. We can find them by converting to decimals or using the formula (a+b)/2.

questions.text: Every natural number is a whole number.
questions.explanation: Natural numbers (1, 2, 3, ...) are a subset of whole numbers (0, 1, 2, 3, ...). Every natural number is included in the set of whole numbers.
1/7 = 0.142857142857... The block 142857 repeats infinitely. This is a non-terminating recurring decimal.
